name: Stuffed Pork Chops
recipeInstructions:
1. In a food processor or blender, process the chopped apples, 4 tablespoons of butter, sugar, vanilla, and nutmeg until smooth (about 3-4 minutes). Set aside.
2. In a small bowl, combine the seasoning mix ingredients: salt, onion powder, cayenne pepper, garlic powder, white pepper, dry mustard, rubbed sage, ground cumin, black pepper, dried thyme leaves. Set aside.
3. Prepare the pork chops by cutting a pocket into each chop to hold the stuffing.
4. In a large skillet, brown the ground pork in the remaining 3 tablespoons butter over high heat (about 3 minutes). Add onions, bell peppers, garlic, and 2 tablespoons of the seasoning mix; cook for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally and scraping pan bottom well.
5. Stir in green chilies and their juice, then continue cooking until mixture is well browned (about 6-8 minutes), stirring occasionally and scraping pan bottom as needed. Add stock and cook 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
6. Stir in bread crumbs and cook about 2 more minutes, stirring constantly and scraping pan bottom as needed. Remove from heat.
7. Sprinkle the remaining seasoning mix evenly on both sides of the chops and inside the pockets, pressing it in by hand. Arrange chops with pocket side up in an ungreased baking dish. Spoon stuffing into each pocket; reserve the remaining stuffing.
8. Bake pork chops at 400°F until meat is done (about 1 hour 10 minutes). Place remaining stuffing in a small pan in the oven for last 20 minutes to reheat.
9. Serve immediately with pork chops arranged on top of portions of the remaining stuffing.
